data.title: Asia-Pacific markets mostly rise following quiet day on Wall Street; eyes on more earnings
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly higher on Wednesday, diverging from the trends observed in major Wall Street indices. The impressive market debut of Tokyo Metro, a Japanese subway operator, further enhanced investor confidence.

The Japan (NKY:IND) fall -0.67% to below 3,200 on Wednesday, reversing gains from earlier in the session as caution dominated sentiment ahead of Japan’s general election this weekend. The Japanese yen past 151.5 per dollar.

A leading Tokyo subway operator raised 348.6 billion yen in its largest IPO since 2018, reportedly 15 times oversubscribed and priced at 1,200 yen apiece, making it the largest IPO in Japan since 2018.

Traders looked forward to the general election this weekend, with polls suggesting that the ruling Liberal Democratic Party could lose its majority with coalition partner Komeito.

China (SHCOMP) rose +0.79% to around 3,300 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.3% to 10,590 on Wednesday, with mainland stocks rising for the fourth straight session as the impact of Beijing's stimulus measures take shape.

Shares of drinks maker China Resources Beverage jumped 13.5% on its Hong Kong trading debut.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +1.70% to 20,647 in early deals on Wednesday, increasing for the second session as gains were widespread across all sectors. Traders were upbeat after some local financial institutions in China Tuesday tapped a swap facility from the PBoC to revive the stock market.

India (SENSEX) rose +0.21%

Australia (AS51) rose +0.10% to around 8,230 on Wednesday, recouping some losses from the previous session, with mining and energy stocks leading the rebound amid firmer commodity prices.

In the U.S., on Tuesday, all three major indexes ended mixed amid rising interest rate concerns and mixed earnings results.

Investors now look ahead to earnings reports from major firms such as AT&T, Boeing and Tesla on Wednesday.

U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday as investors navigated concerns about rising Treasury yields and mixed earnings results: Dow -0.24%; S&P 500 -0.06%; Nasdaq -0.09%.

Investors now look ahead to the Fed’s Beige Book summary of economic conditions on Wednesday for updates on the central bank’s economic outlook. Market participants are also closely monitoring the upcoming US presidential election, which remains highly competitive.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's inflation rate slows to 2.5% in September

China's Q3 GDP figures point at a slowing economy

Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%

Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months

data.title: ProQR Therapeutics prices $75M offering and enters $12M private placement
data.description:
• ProQR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:PRQR) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 18M ordinary shares at a public offering price of $3.50 per share.
  • Total gross proceeds are expected to be $63M.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.7M additional ordinary shares at the public offering price.
  • Concurrently with the offering, ProQR (PRQR) entered into a share purchase agreement with Eli Lilly and Company, one of its existing shareholders to purchase, ~3.52M shares for total gross proceeds of ~$12.3M.
  • Net proceeds together with its existing cash and cash equivalents will be used to primarily fund research and development and clinical development to support the advancement of its current or future product candidates and the expansion of its research and development programs, and the remainder for working capital, capital expenditures and other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price dropped 4.8% on Tuesday during after hours of trading.

data.title: Founder Group prices IPO at $4 per share
data.description:
• Founder Group (FGL) priced its initial public offering of an aggregate 1,218,750 ordinary shares at a public offering price of $4.00 per share.
  • Total gross proceeds are expected to be $4.875M.
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 182,813 ordinary shares at the IPO price.
  • Founder Group is a pure-play, end-to-end EPCC solutions provider for solar PV facilities in Malaysia.

data.title: Wednesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications fell for the third week, ending 17% lower for the week ending October 11 after the benchmark 30-year fixed mortgage rate climbed another 16 bps to 6.52%.

9:00 AM Fed’s Bowman Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Michelle Bowman to give opening remarks on second day of the Eighth Annual Fintech Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.

10:00 AM Existing Home Sales

Existing Home Sales measures the change in the annualized number of existing residential buildings that were sold during the previous month. This report helps to gauge the strength of the U.S. housing market and is a key indicator of overall economic strength. Existing home sales is expected to be marginally higher to 3.88M in September after declining 2.5% to 3.86 million in August of 2024.

10:00 AM Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations

The Atlanta Fed's Business Inflation Expectations survey provides a monthly measure of year-ahead inflation expectations and inflation uncertainty from the perspective of firms. The survey also provides a monthly gauge of firms' current sales, profit margins, and unit cost changes.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories decreased by 2.19 million barrels in the last week.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

The U.S. Treasury will announce buyback at 11:00 AM. Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

12:00 PM Fed’s Barkin: "Recognizing the Impact of Community Colleges"

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin to speak on "Recognizing the Impact of Community Colleges" before the 2024 Virginia Education and Workforce Conference

1:00 PM 20-Yr Bond Auction

The treasury will hold an auction of $13B, 19-Year 10-Month bond.

2:00 PM Fed's Beige Book

The Federal Reserve's Beige Book is a report on current economic conditions in each of the 12 Federal districts in the U.S. It gives a picture of economic trends and challenges in the U.S. It is released 8 times a year, 2 weeks before each Federal Open Market Committee meeting. The report is used by the FOMC in their decision on short-term interest rates.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, Treasury publishes summary results.

Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: What investors need to know about the McDonald's E. coli outbreak
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) disclosed on Tuesday that initial findings from an investigation into an E. coli outbreak indicate that a subset of illnesses may be linked to slivered onions used in the Quarter Pounder and sourced by a single supplier that serves three distribution centers. The restaurant chain said that in line with its safety protocols, all local restaurants have been instructed to remove the product from their supply, and McDonald's (MCD) has paused the distribution of all slivered onions in the impacted area. The Chicago-based company also temporarily removed the Quarter Pounder from restaurants in the impacted area, including Colorado, Kansas, Utah and Wyoming, as well as portions of Idaho, Iowa, Missouri, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Mexico, and Oklahoma.

The CDC said the outbreak involved 49 incidents across ten states, with one death and 10 hospitalizations being reported. The person who died after contracting E. coli was described by Colordao health officials as being "older" and with underlying conditions.

On Wall Street, TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles said the firm's quick franchisee checks suggested McDonald's (MCD) is on top of the issue and that it seems largely contained. Charles said it is only natural to expect a short-term impact on sales. Based on the food contamination analogies of Chipotle (CMG) in 2015 and Jack in the Box (JACK) in 1993, Charles and his team estimate that every 1% annualized change in U.S. same-store sales will lead to a $0.09 EPS impact. TD Cowen sees a worst-case scenario hit to Q4 EPS of $0.37.

Shares of McDonald's (MCD) fell 5.8% in postmarket trading on Tuesday.
